rationale: Bulk write batch splitting existed but failed to account for command overhead, sending oversized commands; this corrects existing splitting behavior, a CRUD bulkWrite conformance bug.

Unacknowledged (w:0) bulk write returns a full result document instead of the spec-required empty result; this is a CRUD result-shape violation in an existing bulk operation implementation. Though it uses the C driver's bulk API, the bug is about the result shape (BulkWriteResult contents) defined by the CRUD spec.
Root cause is that count_with_opts ignores read preferences, sending the operation to the wrong server—a read-preference/server-selection routing violation rather than a CRUD result-shape issue.

find_with_opts incorrectly rejects valid $slice projection operators via BSON_VALIDATE_DOLLAR_KEYS; this is an existing find() implementation behaving incorrectly vs CRUD spec which permits $ projection operators.
Driver's find/query implementation incorrectly rejects valid queries mixing $ and non-$ operators; this is a behavioral defect in existing query handling, a CRUD result/semantics violation.
Aggregate with secondary read preference fails with 'not master' because the driver doesn't route to a secondary / set slaveOk — a server-selection routing bug, not a CRUD result-shape issue.

Bug is in legacy mongoc_collection_find constructing a malformed find wire command (stripping $ prefix, sending 'or' instead of filter:{$or}); this is wire-command construction of the find command, not the CRUD-spec API, and the legacy API is explicitly out of CRUD scope.
The bug is that batchSize is not applied to subsequent getMore batches, only the first batch—this is cursor iteration/getMore batch-sizing mechanics, not the initial aggregate call shape.

delete_one/delete_many existed but failed to send collation inside the delete entry (sent as command option or omitted); the operation existed but behaved incorrectly per the CRUD spec's collation placement rule.

CRUD spec quoted directly: driver MUST raise error when arrayFilters used on servers <3.6; the update operations existed but failed to perform this required validation check, so it's a conformance bug.

The mongoc_bulk_operation_update_*_with_opts() functions are the C driver's legacy bulk API, not the CRUD-spec-defined updateOne/updateMany; per scope rules, bugs in these old APIs (mongoc_bulk_operation_update family) are not CRUD spec nonconformances—but the arrayFilters option semantics are CRUD-governed, making this borderline. Marking conformance_bug as the option existed and was broken by refactoring.

The bulk write models per CRUD spec were never intended to support bypassDocumentValidation on individual insert/update/replace operations; the driver incorrectly accepted and sometimes applied it, a CRUD option-semantics violation in existing code.

The bug is that aggregate with $out fails to inherit the collection writeConcern when a bare BSON array is passed—a CRUD aggregate option-semantics violation in existing code, not malformed writeConcern document construction.

arrayFilters option for bulkWrite existed but was serialized as BSON document type instead of array type, a regression in existing CRUD operation behavior caught by bulkWrite-arrayFilters.yml; this is the high-level CRUD option semantics, not a generic BSON encoding bug.
The bug is that the driver constructs the getMore wire command with a writeConcern field, which getMore does not support; this is a wire-command-format violation, not a CRUD result-shape issue.
countDocuments existed but failed to always prepend the $match stage when no filter was provided, violating the CRUD spec's countDocuments aggregation pipeline construction rule.

CRUD spec's 'ordered' option requires unordered bulk writes to continue after a write error; the existing implementation incorrectly set must_stop=true on any error, a behavioral violation of existing code.

Root cause is the retry machinery: after a retryable write selects a new server, the bulk loop reuses the old server id rather than the newly selected server. This is a retryable-writes rule about which server to use after retry, not a CRUD result-shape issue.
Root cause is a sessions spec rule: estimatedDocumentCount must not accept an explicit session parameter, cited directly from the sessions spec.
estimatedDocumentCount existed but returned inconsistent reply format across server versions; the spec only requires returning an int, so this corrects existing wrong result-shape behavior.

The driver had arrayFilters tracking but failed to set has_array_filters for subsequent update ops in a bulk, so it didn't raise the client-side error required by the CRUD spec on unsupported servers; this corrects existing buggy behavior.

Bulk write spec (part of CRUD) requires coalescing consecutive same-type operations into batches; the driver had bulk update/delete implemented but failed to coalesce them, producing extra roundtrips—an existing-behavior conformance bug, not a missing implementation.
countDocuments existed but the hint option was unvalidated and bypassed proper opts handling; CRUD spec requires countDocuments to support hint, so this corrects existing wrong behavior.

This concerns mongoc_bulkwrite_execute / mongoc_bulkwritereturn_t — the new MongoClient.bulkWrite() API (DRIVERS-3184, client bulk write), not Collection.bulkWrite(); the partialResult/BulkWriteException rule is from the bulk-write spec.
The Collection.bulkWrite let option is silently dropped when the document exceeds ~91 bytes; existing bulk write code mishandles the let option, a CRUD conformance bug.

The bug is that a bulk write loses its server hint and re-runs server selection, routing to the wrong node (e.g. primary instead of intended secondary local db); this is a routing/server-selection violation, not a CRUD result-shape issue.

nInserted (BulkWriteResult.insertedCount) is a CRUD result-shape field; counting server-rejected documents is incorrect existing behavior in mongoc_bulk_operation_insert, making this a conformance bug.
Bulk write result omits nRemoved field when no remove ops executed; CRUD spec requires all BulkWriteResult fields always present, so existing implementation behaves incorrectly vs spec.

Ordered bulk write must halt after a failure (oversized doc); driver incorrectly continued as if unordered — a CRUD bulk-write ordering semantics violation in an existing implementation. Note it involves mongoc_bulk_operation_t which is the C driver bulk API; ordering semantics are CRUD-governed but legacy-API caveat lowers confidence.

Bug is in mongoc_bulk_operation_t which fails to split large inserts under the max message size limit, a CRUD bulk-write batch-splitting rule; the operation existed but behaved incorrectly. Note this uses the C driver's legacy bulk API, which weakens the CRUD-spec fit.
Bulk write result reports wrong writeErrors index (0 instead of 1) for the failing second op; this is a CRUD result-shape (WriteError.index) violation in existing code.

The fix targets legacy APIs (mongoc_collection_insert/update/remove) which are pre-CRUD-spec APIs; however the bug concerns error.domain consistency for oversized BSON documents, which is more about BSON error classification than CRUD result-shape. Tagging crud as the prior did, but with reduced confidence since these are legacy methods not the CRUD-spec updateOne/insertOne family.

The bug is that wtimeout WriteConcernError is not surfaced in the bulk write result's writeConcernError array as spec'ed — a CRUD result-shape violation (failure to populate writeConcernErrors), not a malformed writeConcern sent to server. The merge code existed but behaved incorrectly for legacy writes.
The bulk write result omits the spec-required writeErrors field (should be empty array on success); this is a CRUD result-shape violation in existing bulk write code.

The bug is that the BulkWriteResult fails to populate writeConcernErrors correctly (wrong field name singular vs plural, wrong array structure) — a CRUD result-shape violation; the operation existed but produced incorrect result format.
This concerns mongoc_collection_insert_bulk, a legacy pre-CRUD-spec API; legacy insert batching is not a CRUD spec nonconformance. Reclassified out of crud scope, so bug_type is not_applicable.
This is the deprecated legacy insert API (mongoc_collection_insert_bulk) using legacy OP_INSERT, which is explicitly outside CRUD spec scope; not a CRUD spec method.

The aggregate() operation existed but misbehaved by not checking maxWireVersion to construct the cursor field correctly, causing double execution; this is incorrect behavior of an existing CRUD operation (aggregate command construction), corrected here.

Legacy getLastError path (_mongoc_write_result_merge_legacy) skips an error lacking a 'code', so an oversized bulk insert is wrongly reported as successful — a CRUD result-shape error (writeErrors not surfaced). This is a conformance fix to existing bulk-write merge logic, not a write-concern document construction issue.

Ticket cites CRUD spec AggregateOptions.batchSize not being sent to server; though resolved as works-as-designed, the alleged defect is a CRUD option-handling conformance issue in existing aggregate implementation.

The bulk write result fails to surface write concern errors (bson_error_t not populated from writeConcernErrors), a CRUD result-shape violation per the critical distinction; the operation existed but behaved incorrectly.

The bug is that the driver generates malformed BSON array encoding for the insert command's documents field, using duplicate key '0' instead of incrementing keys '0','1','2'. This is a BSON serialization defect, not a CRUD result-shape or option-semantics issue.
find_one_and_replace/update exist but fail to validate update/replace documents for valid keys as the CRUD spec's update-vs-replace-validation rules require; existing behavior corrected.
Existing CRUD operations explicitly set ordered=false, but the spec default should be true; the operations already existed and behaved incorrectly, making this a conformance bug.

Existing CRUD result/option fields (bulk_write inserted_count, update matched_count, find limit/skip) use int32 where CRUD spec requires int64; the operations existed but had wrong field types, making this a conformance fix.

find_one_and_* operations exist but crash when reading a non-existent reply value under unacknowledged write concern; should return no document instead. This is a CRUD result-handling conformance bug, not write-concern document construction.
aggregate() existed and supported readConcern since 3.5.0 (CXX-1702), but the append method silently omitted the readConcern option—an existing CRUD aggregate operation behaving incorrectly vs. spec.

Bug is in the legacy Query API (pre-CRUD-spec) on the legacy branch, dealing with the maxTimeMS query option field name; this is not a CRUD-spec method nor a wire-protocol find command spec violation.
The aggregate() operation existed but incorrectly omitted the required 'cursor' key in the command, violating the CRUD spec's useCursor behavior; this corrects existing wrong behavior.
Resolution confirms the spec actually requires throwing on empty bulk operations; the fix was only documentation, not a behavior change—this is not a CRUD nonconformance bug at all.
findOneAndReplace existed but failed to validate that the replacement doc must not contain atomic operators, a CRUD spec update-document validation rule; fix adds the missing error.

CRUD spec requires writeErrors[].index to reflect position in the original bulk operation array; existing bulkWrite code returned position within writeErrors array instead, a result-shape conformance bug.
BulkWriteError result shape inconsistent: writeErrors field missing for single-error case in bulkWrite/insertMany result, a CRUD result-shape violation in existing code.
Unordered bulkWrite must continue executing all batches after errors per CRUD spec; a refactoring removed the check causing abort on first error, an existing implementation behaving incorrectly.

Bulk update operations existed but failed to validate that update documents contain atomic operators (rejecting replacement docs), a CRUD spec update-validation rule; commit asserts atomic operator requirements across crud and bulk operations.
BulkWriteResult.insertedIds returns undefined values instead of the generated _ids, a CRUD result-shape violation in an existing bulkWrite implementation (inverted boolean for server id generation).
BulkWriteResult properties become out of sync with BulkResult, meaning the CRUD result-shape fields (insertedCount, modifiedCount, etc.) report wrong values from an existing implementation.

Bulk execute() called twice should raise a descriptive error per the bulk spec but silently returns; this is existing behavior that regressed ('lost through refactoring'), making it a conformance bug in the CRUD/bulkWrite area.

[manual correction] Driver has explain support but incorrectly rejects arbitrary explain verbosity levels that the spec (DRIVERS-1306) requires be allowed; this corrects existing wrong validation behavior.

[manual correction] Explain verbosity is a CRUD operation option; the driver had explain implemented but incorrectly rejected arbitrary explain levels via validation, which is a behavior correction not a new feature.

The find() sort option exists but doesn't preserve the order of fields specified in an array-form sort; the CRUD spec governs the sort option semantics for find, making this a conformance bug in existing code.
findOne existed but returned undefined instead of null when no result found, violating the CRUD spec's findOne result behavior; this ticket corrects the existing wrong behavior.

Bug only manifests when limit exceeds the batch size (1000), indicating the driver mishandles limit accounting across getMore cursor iterations rather than the initial find() call shape — a cursor iteration/batch-sizing defect.
[manual correction] listDatabases nameOnly option exists but is ignored, sending NaN instead of the user-specified boolean; existing implementation behaves incorrectly vs. the option semantics.

insertMany returned malformed insertedIds (double-mapped array instead of index->_id mapping), violating the CRUD spec's InsertManyResult.insertedIds shape; the operation existed but behaved incorrectly.
Concerns the shape of UpdateResult.upsertedId returned by updateOne; the value form deviated from expected behavior and was reverted, a CRUD result-shape conformance issue.
insertMany with ordered:false returns N+1 insertedIds (extra null) due to 1-indexed internal representation; this is a CRUD InsertManyResult.insertedIds result-shape violation in existing code.
[manual correction] count() existed but ignored the hint option, failing to forward it to the count command; the fix corrected existing buggy behavior.

CRUD spec's update-vs-replace validation requires replace operations to prohibit pipelines; FindOneAndReplace and BulkWrite replaceOne already implemented but incorrectly allowed pipelines, a conformance defect being corrected.

The count operation's hint option exists but incorrectly converts a key pattern document to an index name string instead of passing it as-is; this is a CRUD operation option-semantics violation in existing code.
CRUD spec requires updateMany to validate that update documents contain atomic operators ($set, etc.); the bulkWrite() updateMany path failed to enforce this validation rule.
Aggregate operation existed but incorrectly applied batchSize=0 with $out stage; CRUD spec requires batchSize be ignored for $out aggregations, so this corrects existing wrong behavior.

Adds pipeline-style update support to Collection.bulkWrite update model — a CRUD spec feature that was entirely absent for bulk writes (overlooked in PHPLIB-418), so this is the first implementation rather than a correction of existing behavior.
CRUD spec states upsert/returnDocument(new) sent only if caller explicitly provides them; existing findAndModify implementation always sent both, violating the optional-parameter semantics.
The aggregate operation existed but incorrectly defaulted allowDiskUse to false and sent it, violating the CRUD spec rule that optional fields must be unset unless explicitly provided.
Update methods existed but used the wrong 'limit' option instead of 'multi' for update operations, a CRUD spec semantics violation corrected in existing code.

The bug is in the hint+unacknowledged-write validation guard for FindAndModify (a CRUD operation); isAcknowledgedWriteConcern incorrectly classified w=1 and string write concerns, an existing logic error in CRUD-level option validation, not in how the writeConcern document is sent to the server.
count() returns float 128.0 instead of integer 128; the operation existed but produced wrong result type vs. CRUD spec's integer count result, fixed by casting to integer.

InsertOneResult.insertedId/InsertManyResult.insertedIds result shape existed but only tracked generated IDs; CRUD spec requires these always populated regardless of generation — a result-shape conformance fix.
insert_many existed but raised NoMethodError instead of a proper MaxBSONSize error when a document exceeds size limit; incorrect error handling in an existing CRUD operation is a conformance bug.

insert_many returns only 625 of 10000 inserted_ids; CRUD spec requires InsertManyResult.insertedIds contain all inserted ids. Existing implementation was buggy in combining inserted/upserted ids across bulk batches.

BulkWrite::Result returned incorrect n_upserted and n_matched counts (existing implementation produced wrong values), violating the CRUD spec's BulkWriteResult shape; the fix corrects the count aggregation logic.

The bug is in the legacy update operation path (Mongo::Operation::Update::Legacy) where the upsert option isn't forwarded due to a string/symbol key mismatch. This is a legacy pre-CRUD-spec OP_UPDATE wire path rather than the CRUD-spec updateOne method, but it surfaces through update_one; given the legacy-API exclusion this is borderline, though the user-facing update_one option semantics are CRUD-governed.
estimatedDocumentCount already existed but silently dropped filter conditions and returned wrong count; CRUD spec requires no filter support, so this corrects existing incorrect behavior.

Ticket implements update-vs-replace validation (atomic operator requirements) per the CRUD spec URL; this validation was entirely absent before ('Implement update and replace validation'), making it an initial implementation of a required feature.

Root cause is incorrect txn_num incrementing during bulk write batch-splitting inside a transaction, violating transaction-level invariants; the comment confirms incrementing should only happen when not in a transaction.

Find-related options (readConcern, collation, etc.) exist but aren't correctly propagated to find/aggregate operations; this corrects existing option-forwarding behavior, a CRUD result/option-shape conformance issue.
Summary 'Make #find_one_and_delete a method on Collection' and commit 'Add #find_one_and_delete as a method' indicate the operation was entirely absent and added for the first time.
Ticket adds find_one_and_replace as a new Collection method with its options for the first time ('Make #find_one_and_replace a method'), an initial implementation of a CRUD spec operation.

parallel_scan is not part of the CRUD spec's defined read operations (find, aggregate, count, distinct, etc.); it's a separate parallelCollectionScan feature, so it falls under 'other' rather than crud.
Summary 'Make #find_one_and_update a method on Collection' and commit 'Add #find_one_and_update as a method' indicate the operation was entirely absent and added for the first time.
Adds #aggregate, #count, #distinct as Collection methods per CRUD spec; these were absent and added for the first time, so initial_implementation.
aggregate already existed on Collection but lacked required CRUD spec options (allow_disk_use, batch_size, max_time_ms, use_cursor); this ticket adds the missing options to existing operations.
Adds maxTimeMS option support to Collection#count, a CRUD read operation option that was previously absent; 'Support max_time_ms option' indicates first-time addition.
Summary 'Support max_time_ms option with Collection#distinct' indicates the maxTimeMS option was absent from distinct and is being added for the first time, a CRUD spec option/feature addition.
cursorType is a CRUD-spec find() option (NON_TAILABLE/TAILABLE/TAILABLE_WAIT); summary 'Support cursorType' indicates the option was being added for the first time.
This is the #update method on a users view (user management / updateUser), not a CRUD Collection operation. User management is governed by a separate spec, not the CRUD spec.
Ticket ensures Collection#find supports all CRUD spec required options (allowPartialResults, comment, cursorType, etc.), adding option support that was absent rather than fixing existing behavior.
Summary 'Make #delete_one a method on Collection' and commit 'Add #delete_one as a method' indicate the CRUD operation was entirely absent and added for the first time.
Summary 'Make #delete_many a method on Collection' and commit 'Add #delete_many as a method' indicate the CRUD operation was entirely absent and added for the first time.
Summary 'Make #replace_one a method on Collection' and commit 'Add #replace_one as a method' indicate the replace_one operation was entirely absent and added for the first time.
Summary 'Make #update_many a method on Collection' and commit 'Add #update_many as a method' indicate the spec-required operation was entirely absent and added for the first time.
Summary 'Make #update_one a method on Collection' and commit 'Add #update_one as a method' indicate the CRUD-spec operation was entirely absent and added for the first time.

BulkWrite with w:10 swallows the write-concern/operation failure and returns a result instead of surfacing the error; failing to surface writeConcernError in a CRUD bulk result is a CRUD result-shape conformance bug in existing code.
Driver fails to check 'hidden' field, routing reads to hidden secondaries—a server selection/routing rule violation, not a CRUD result-shape or semantics issue.
Bug is about wire-level wrapping of read preference ($query must wrap empty filter when $readPreference present), governed by Server Selection Spec, not CRUD result/option semantics.
Bug is about wire-level message length validation of the find command response (16mb limit) after upgrading to find commands, a wire protocol encoding concern rather than CRUD API result shape.
Bug is about ReadConcern inheritance from Client/Database/Collection parent hierarchy, governed by the read-concern spec, not CRUD operation result shapes or options.

Bug is about misinterpreting little-endian wire protocol header bytes on big-endian systems (OP_REPLY response_to parsing), a binary encoding/decoding issue, not a CRUD result-shape or option semantics violation.

Comment cites the CRUD spec Update vs. Replace Validation rule; the bug concerns whether update operations require $ operators, a CRUD update-document-validation rule, though it touches the legacy bulk API.
Bug is about the wire-level BSON type of batchSize (int64 vs int32) in find/getMore commands, which is governed by the find-getmore-killcursors wire-protocol spec, not the high-level CRUD API.

Bug is that generic command functions wrongly apply the client's read/write concern automatically, violating the read-write-concern spec's generic RunCommand rule; not a CRUD operation result-shape issue.
Bug is that a 'create' command was routed to a secondary instead of primary, violating the Server Selection Spec's rule that command helpers must ignore read preference and use primary.
Bug is in bson_iter_visit_all's handling of invalid UTF-8 keys during BSON iteration/decoding, a BSON-level concern, not a CRUD API result shape or operation semantic.
Documentation example fix on the libbson reference page, not a driver spec nonconformance; Sonnet's prior dismissal is correct.
Build system change syncing CMakeLists.txt with autotools for Secure Transport TLS support; no CRUD spec involvement.
Precondition failure in _mongoc_n_return() relates to cursor batch sizing/remaining-count mechanics, not CRUD result shape or find option API; not a CRUD spec nonconformance.

Bug is in how the driver constructs the getMore wire command's batchSize field (should send MIN(batchSize, limit-numberReceived)), which is wire-level getMore command format, not the CRUD-level find API result/option shape.
Segfault in X.509 authentication when username extraction fails; this is an auth-mechanism crash, not a CRUD spec result-shape or operation semantics issue.

Bug is about single-threaded server selection timeout timing (sleep intervals between server checks, serverSelectionTimeoutMS error message), which is governed by the server selection spec, not CRUD result shapes.
Bug is about mongoc_cursor_is_alive() returning wrong liveness state during cursor iteration across batches, a cursor lifecycle mechanics issue, not a CRUD result-shape or API violation.
Bug is about libbson serializing BSON Code/Code-with-scope type to Extended JSON ($code/$scope), a BSON serialization issue, not a CRUD API shape or semantics violation.

find_and_modify result fails to surface writeConcernError as a failure; per the critical distinction, failing to surface a WriteConcernError in a CRUD result object is a CRUD result-shape violation, and the operation already existed.
Bug is about Extended JSON serialization order of $binary/$type fields in libbson, a BSON encoding/decoding concern, not a CRUD API shape or semantics issue.
Bug is in mongoc_topology_description_suitable_servers read-preference/tag-set selection logic for SecondaryPreferred; this is a server selection spec violation, not CRUD result shape.
Bug is in handshake metadata document size check during isMaster/topology scanning, governed by the handshake spec, not CRUD operations.
Bug is about libbson parsing MongoDB Extended JSON ($numberLong) inconsistently at the top level — a BSON extended JSON encoding/decoding issue, not a CRUD API shape or semantics issue.
JSON-to-BSON conversion narrows negative int64 to int32 due to a missing INT32_MIN bound check; this is a BSON encoding bug, not a CRUD API/result-shape issue.
Flaky mock-server test failure fixed by mock server reliability commit; no driver spec behavior change.
Bug is in tag set matching logic during server selection, causing wrong server count; this is a server-selection spec rule violation, not CRUD.
Use-after-free crash in test mockserver code, not a driver bug; reporter states 'I don't think this was ever a real bug in the driver' and closed as unreproducible. No CRUD spec rule violated.

This adds a private function to append the writeConcern document to drop/createCollection commands when wire version >= 5; it concerns how the driver constructs and sends write concern to the server, not CRUD result shapes.
Bug is about libbson UTF-8 string validation/encoding (0xE9 byte handling), a BSON serialization concern, not a CRUD API shape or semantics issue.

Concerns parsing writeConcernError for admin/DDL commands (collection_drop, create_collection, drop_index, database_drop) ending in '_with_write_concern', not CRUD operations. These commands are outside CRUD spec scope, so this is a write-concern handling issue.
Bug is about driver incorrectly treating PossiblePrimary servers as candidates for primary reads, a Server Selection spec routing violation, not a CRUD result-shape or semantics issue.

Bug is about cursor iteration mechanics: empty getMore response incorrectly marks tailable cursor as 'done', breaking getMore lifecycle. This is cursor exhaustion/iteration behavior, not the find/aggregate call shape or result type.

Bug is in the driver's write concern validation logic incorrectly rejecting valid 'majority' string and tag sets when constructing the writeConcern document; this is write-concern spec, not CRUD result shape.

Bug is about the driver duplicating/mis-applying readConcern and writeConcern fields in the command document sent to the server (how the WC/RC document is constructed), which is write-concern/read-concern, not a CRUD result-shape violation.

Bug is that setting cursor hint incorrectly sets SlaveOkay bit via mongos, changing read preference to secondaryPreferred—a read-preference/routing issue, not a CRUD result-shape or option-semantics violation.

aggregate() is a CRUD read operation; the bug is that it fails to inherit collection read/write concern and apply option semantics correctly, a CRUD result/option-shape conformance issue in an existing implementation.
Bug is about BSON boolean byte validation (0x01 vs any non-zero) in bson_validate/bson_iter_bool — pure BSON encoding/decoding, not CRUD API shape or semantics.
Bug is about libbson failing to validate the inner length prefix of BSON binary subtype 0x02 during deserialization—a BSON encoding validation issue, not a CRUD API shape or semantics issue.
Bug is about BSON deserialization validation of DBPointer collection name null-termination, a BSON binary format issue, not a CRUD API shape or semantics issue.
localThresholdMS unit mismatch affects which server is selected for secondary reads; this is a server-selection spec violation, not a CRUD result-shape issue.

Bug is about iterating command cursors with legacy OP_GET_MORE wire protocol instead of the getMore command on modern servers—a wire-level getMore command format issue, not a CRUD result/API shape issue.
Bug is in the JSON parser accepting malformed JSON (stray '}'), a BSON/extended JSON parsing concern, not a CRUD API shape or semantics issue.

This concerns the legacy mongoc_collection_update() API, which is a pre-CRUD-spec driver method explicitly excluded from CRUD spec scope; the CRUD spec defines updateOne/updateMany, not this legacy API.
Bug is in extended JSON datetime parsing (bson_new_from_json) failing the BSON corpus datetime.json test; this is BSON serialization/parsing, not a CRUD API shape or semantics issue.
Bug is about how bson_as_json encodes a double value into JSON (extended JSON / bson-corpus spec, SPEC-587), not about CRUD API shape or semantics.

Bug is about the wire-level getMore command containing a prohibited readConcern field, causing server error 'Unrecognized field readConcern'; this is getMore command construction, not CRUD result/API shape.
Bug is about localThresholdMS=0 being ignored, affecting which server is selected by RTT—directly a server-selection spec rule, not a CRUD result/option issue.

Bug is about Collection.bulkWrite()-family document validation (replace/update atomic-operator and BSON validity checks) and consistent error surfacing, which the CRUD spec governs; existing code validated inconsistently and swallowed errors, a conformance defect.
Bug concerns validation of the localThresholdMS URI option, which governs server selection latency window, not a CRUD operation result shape or semantics.
Bug is localThresholdMS default value (should be 15ms not 15s), a server-selection spec rule affecting which servers are eligible for selection; not a CRUD result-shape or operation issue.
Bug is about cursor exhaustion mechanics (mongoc_cursor_is_alive/more returning incorrect state after exhaustion), not about CRUD find() result shape or options.
Build/linker issue with pkg-config files referencing crypt32.lib under MinGW-64; no CRUD spec behavior involved.

Bug is in single-threaded server selection scanning order (SDAM/server-selection spec): driver blocks for minHeartbeatFrequencyMS instead of aborting early when no nodes are scannable due to cooldownMS. No CRUD result-shape or operation semantics involved.
This is a MongoDB Handshake spec violation (appName not sent on pooled client sockets), not a CRUD spec result-shape or operation-semantics issue.
Bug is about BSON-to-JSON string escaping for regex/symbol/DBPointer types, governed by the BSON corpus/extended JSON spec, not CRUD API shape or semantics.
Bug is about the JSON reader's ability to parse legacy $date integer values (Extended JSON parsing), not about any CRUD operation API or result shape. Sonnet's bson classification is correct.
Bug is about BSON UTF-8 validation rejecting null bytes in string values (missing BSON_VALIDATE_UTF_ALLOW_NULL), which is BSON serialization/validation, not CRUD API shape or semantics.
Bug is about OP_MSG document sequence length being written in native instead of little-endian, a wire-protocol encoding issue unrelated to CRUD API shape or semantics.
Bug is that a read-write command obeys read preference and selects a secondary instead of routing to primary; this is server-selection routing, not a CRUD result-shape issue.
Bug is in bson_decimal128_to_string emitting 35 digits instead of 34 per Decimal128 spec, writing past buffer end; this is a BSON serialization bug, not a CRUD API shape/semantics issue.
Bug is about excluding arbiters when determining cluster session support (logicalSessionTimeoutMinutes), a sessions spec rule, not a CRUD result-shape or operation issue.
Documentation-only fix for RST pages describing command_with_opts and aggregate option parsing; no driver behavior change.

Bug is about the driver failing to append the client's writeConcern document TO the server for findAndModify on wire version 4, a write-concern construction/sending issue, not a CRUD result-shape violation.

Bug is about causal consistency/afterClusterTime being added to generic runCommand commands that don't support readConcern, governed by sessions/causal-consistency spec, not CRUD result shapes or operation semantics.

Bug is about the generic mongoc_client_command_with_opts helper ignoring an explicit readConcern option for raw commands; this is read-concern/command-execution behavior, not a CRUD operation result-shape or semantics violation.
Root cause is retry machinery improperly attaching txnNumber/lsid to unacknowledged (w:0) writes, governed by the Retryable Writes and Sessions specs, not by CRUD result shape or operation semantics.
Bug is about $clusterTime gossiping to standalone servers, governed by causal-consistency/sessions spec, not a CRUD result-shape or operation-semantics issue.

Directly cites the sessions spec rule that getMore must reuse the same session ID as the originating operation; the bug is implicit session reuse across cursor iteration, not a CRUD result shape or option violation.
Bug is about BSON binary value handling (empty binary with NULL data asserts, UBSAN in bson_value_copy), purely serialization/encoding-level, not CRUD API shape or semantics.
Change stream forgets implicit session lsid between aggregate and getMore; this is a sessions-spec violation (same lsid must be used), not a CRUD result-shape or option bug.
Driver appends lsid to authenticate commands, violating the sessions spec rule that session IDs must not be sent with authenticate commands; not a CRUD result-shape or operation issue.

Root cause is the Driver Sessions spec rule that drivers MUST NOT send lsid with unacknowledged writes and SHOULD raise an error for explicit sessions; this is a session-handling/lsid-attachment violation, not a CRUD result-shape issue.
Bug is that aggregate fails to inherit collection read preference for server selection, routing the operation using wrong read prefs—a server-selection rule violation, not a CRUD result-shape issue.

Bug is about wire-level command construction for mongos (readConcern duplicated at top level and inside $query wrapper for read preference routing), a command-formatting/read-concern issue, not a CRUD result-shape or option-semantics violation.

The bug is that the cursor code spuriously rejects an explicit primary read preference inside a transaction, violating transaction-spec rules about read preferences in transactions; the CRUD result shape and find() API are unaffected.

Bug is about mongoc_collection_watch (change stream) incorrectly preferring collection readConcern over opts readConcern; this is change-streams spec behavior, not a CRUD operation result shape or semantics.

Bug is about readConcern precedence (user-provided in opts vs collection default) in find_with_opts; this is a read-concern spec inheritance rule, not a CRUD result-shape or operation-semantics violation.
Bug is about createIndex inheriting collection writeConcern, an index-management operation not covered by the CRUD spec; not a CRUD result-shape or write-concern document construction issue.

Bug is about transaction options (readConcern/writeConcern/readPreference) not being inherited from client during startSession/transaction setup; this is a transactions spec inheritance rule, not a CRUD result-shape or operation issue.

Bug is that the driver fails to override collection readConcern with the transaction's readConcern on the first command, violating the Transactions spec's readConcern override rule, not a CRUD result-shape rule.

Driver incorrectly adds txnNumber to multi-document deleteMany/updateMany, violating the retryable-writes spec rule 'MUST NOT add a transaction ID to multi-document write commands'; this is retry machinery misbehavior, not a CRUD result-shape violation.
Bug is about byte-swapping the OP_MSG bitFlags field on big-endian systems—a wire-protocol message encoding issue, not a CRUD API shape or semantics violation.
Bug is about Extended JSON datetime serialization formatting (millisecond zero-padding), a BSON/Extended JSON encoding issue, not a CRUD API shape or semantics violation.
Bug is about bson_oid_init() generating duplicate OIDs after fork due to cached random bytes — a BSON ObjectId generation issue, not a CRUD result-shape or operation-semantics violation.
Bug is in legacy mongoc_collection_update() API (not a CRUD spec method) and the root cause is incorrect txnNumber injection on a multi-write, which violates retryable-writes rules.

Bug is in legacy mongoc_collection_remove/delete APIs failing to set has_multi_write, causing improper txnNumber injection under retryWrites; this is retryable-writes machinery on pre-CRUD-spec legacy methods, not a CRUD result-shape violation.

The resolution clarified that per the server-selection spec, the generic command method MUST ignore database-level read preferences; the fix was a documentation correction, not a CRUD result-shape issue.
This is an implementation bug about the driver aborting on socket failure due to a missing must_stop check in the OP_MSG write path, not a violation of CRUD spec result-shape or semantics rules.
Bug is about mongos pinning/unpinning behavior during transaction commit and transient errors, which is governed by transaction-spec invariants, not CRUD result shapes.

The bug is that the driver inherits the collection's write concern onto commands within a transaction, violating the transactions spec rule that writeConcern must not be added to preceding commands in a transaction—this is a transaction-level invariant violation, not a CRUD result-shape issue.
Bug is about session pinning to mongos for sharded transactions being bypassed when server_id is provided directly; this is a transactions-spec invariant, not a CRUD result-shape issue.
Bug is about URI parsing truncating wtimeoutms to 32-bit, affecting how the driver constructs the write concern value; this is write-concern/connection-string, not a CRUD result-shape issue.
Documentation-only update to clarify MONGOC_QUERY_EXHAUST does not apply to aggregate; no driver behavior change or spec nonconformance.

Bug is about transaction option lifecycle (max_commit_time_ms bleeding between transactions), governed by the transactions spec per-transaction option scoping, not a CRUD result-shape or operation-semantics issue.
Bug is failure to send $readPreference to mongos for secondaryPreferred with maxStalenessSeconds, a server-selection spec rule about read preference transmission, not a CRUD result-shape issue.

Bug is a Max Staleness spec violation (maxWireVersion check must exclude PossiblePrimary servers), affecting server selection/read-preference handling, not a CRUD result-shape or operation-semantics rule.
Bug is about using server descriptions from handshake vs monitoring for wire version checks, governed by the SDAM spec warning—not a CRUD result-shape or operation-semantics violation.
Bug is about how the retryable writes machinery classifies retryable errors (substring matching removed from retryable writes spec), not a CRUD result shape or semantics issue.
Auth commands (GSSAPI/SSPI) were appending session IDs, violating the driver-sessions spec rule that auth commands must not include lsid; not a CRUD result/operation issue.
Bug is about session option defaults (causalConsistency vs snapshot) governed by the Causal Consistency / Sessions specs, not a CRUD result-shape or operation-semantics violation.
Bug is about disabling causal consistency in implicit sessions, governed by the sessions/causal-consistency spec, not CRUD result shapes or operation semantics.
Bug is about retrying operations when the connection handshake fails (retryable reads/writes machinery), not about CRUD result shape; split from DRIVERS-746 retryable handshake errors.
Bug is about propagating the original error when a write is labeled NoWritesPerformed during retry machinery, governed by the retryable-writes spec, not CRUD result shape.
Documentation code snippet fix (error.msg -> error.message and missing brace); not a spec nonconformance.
Bug concerns NoWritesPerformed error handling in the retryable writes error sequence, governed by the retryable-writes spec, not a CRUD result-shape rule.
Segfault in wire-protocol parsing (_mongoc_rpc_scatter) of OP_MSG with optional CRC32C checksum; this is OP_MSG message format handling, not a CRUD API result-shape or option violation.
Bug is about FaaS/AWS Lambda environment detection in handshake metadata, not a CRUD operation result or option; this is the handshake/metadata spec, not CRUD.
Bug is in Decimal128 extJSON parsing causing exponent overflow/underflow in BSON conversion; this is BSON serialization/decoding, not CRUD API shape or semantics.
Bug is in extJSON parsing of $code/$dbPointer arrays producing wrong BSON key bytes; this is BSON serialization, not a CRUD API shape or semantics issue.

Bug is that recoveryToken is not stored/sent for load-balanced topology in commitTransaction/abortTransaction, violating the transactions spec recoveryToken-field rules; not a CRUD result-shape issue.
Bug is about libbson truncating large arrays during BSON deserialization/iteration, a BSON encoding/decoding issue, not a CRUD API result-shape or semantics violation.
Bug concerns handshake metadata (FaaS and container info) in the client handshake, a Handshake spec violation—unrelated to CRUD operations or result shapes.

Crash from a BSON_ASSERT when accessing OP_MSG flag bits before checking the opCode; fix delays flag access until after opCode check. This is a wire-message handling/implementation crash, not a CRUD spec result-shape or semantics violation.

Bug is in legacy mongoc_collection_update() and mongoc_collection_create_bulk_operation() (pre-CRUD-spec legacy APIs), not the new updateOne/updateMany CRUD methods, so it is outside CRUD spec scope; the write concern inheritance issue maps to write-concern.
Bug is about server-selection spec rule for aggregate-with-write: driver checks unavailable Unknown servers when deciding read preference override, directly citing server-selection spec section.

Bug is about write concern inheritance from client to a bulk operation constructed via mongoc_bulk_operation_new() (legacy C API), not about CRUD result shape or surfacing writeConcernErrors; concerns how write concern is propagated/constructed, so write-concern not crud.
Bug is about Extended JSON encoding of date values (numberLong vs ISO string format), a BSON/Extended JSON serialization issue, not a CRUD API shape or semantics violation.
Bug is about URI parsing constructing the write concern document sent to the server (w=1 omitted as default), which is write-concern spec, not CRUD result-shape.
The bug concerns how the driver constructs/validates the write concern document (w=0 with j=true) and whether to send GLE — a write-concern construction issue, not a CRUD result-shape problem.
Memory safety crash (use-after-free of id_iter) in mongoc_client_bulkwrite_execute, the new MongoClient.bulkWrite() 8.0 API; not a CRUD result-shape conformance issue.
Bug is about SS/SDAM timeout units and configurable interval parameters (server-selection/sdam machinery), not CRUD result shapes or operation semantics.
Bug is about validating readPreferenceTags format during URI/connection-string parsing, not about CRUD operation result shapes or semantics.
Bug is about URI/connection-string parsing of invalid w value; resolution confirms connection-string spec requires invalid values to be ignored with a WARN log, not a CRUD result-shape issue.
Bug is in bson_validate's UTF-8 validation logic—how BSON content is validated/decoded—not about CRUD API option, semantics, or result shape. Sonnet's bson classification is correct.
Bug is about createIndexes (index-management spec), where the driver fails to inherit collection write concern and surface writeConcernError. createIndexes is not a CRUD spec operation.

Bug is about validating read preference (invalid tags with primary mode) before server selection, governed by the read preference/server-selection spec, not a CRUD result-shape or operation-semantics issue.

Explicitly described as bugs in the SDAM and Server Selection specs: single-threaded server selection failed to reconnect after error and didn't pause minHeartbeatFrequencyMS between scans. Not a CRUD result-shape issue.

Bug concerns MongoClient.bulkWrite() (client bulkWrite, the 8.0 API) parsing/decoding of unexpected server reply shapes (missing nModified, unexpected cursor results); this is the bulk-write spec, not Collection.bulkWrite() CRUD.
Bug is in server deprioritization retry check, which governs which server an operation routes to during retry — a server-selection rule, not a CRUD result-shape or semantics issue.
Bug is about $clusterTime gossiping (Driver Sessions spec) on single-threaded clients, with the fix implementing Driver Session prose test 3; not a CRUD result-shape or operation-semantics issue.
Bug is about $readPreference being sent to non-mongos nodes, governed by Server Selection spec's 'passing read preference to mongos' rule; cited spec URL confirms this is server-selection, not CRUD.
Bug is that the driver routes queries to a recovering member that should not be selectable for non-PRIMARY reads — a server-selection routing violation, not a CRUD result-shape issue.

Bug is about URI parsing constructing the write concern document (wtimeoutms field) incorrectly, which concerns how the driver builds/sends the write concern—a write-concern spec issue, not CRUD result shape.
Hang/segfault from mishandling EMSGSIZE network write error return; a reliability/socket-layer bug, not a CRUD result-shape or semantics violation.

Bug is that the driver constructs/sends getLastError despite w=0 (unacknowledged) write concern, causing wire protocol errors—a write-concern document handling issue, not a CRUD result-shape violation.
Segfault from bson_destroy on NULL gle after network error in legacy update/delete paths; an internal crash bug in pre-CRUD-spec legacy write APIs, not a CRUD spec nonconformance.
Bug is about validating the wtimeout field of the write concern document (must be >= 0) during URI parsing and set_wtimeout, which is write-concern spec scope, not CRUD result-shape.
Documentation-only task to unify/cross-link installation instructions across READMEs and docs; no CRUD spec behavior involved.
Bug is about JSON→BSON conversion incorrectly turning DBRef objects into DBPointer/ObjectId instead of embedded documents; this is BSON/extended JSON serialization, not CRUD API shape or semantics.

Bug is that the slaveOk wire flag (read preference routing) isn't set when querying a secondary directly via hint, a read-preference/server-selection rule violation, not a CRUD result-shape or API issue. Related to SPEC-352.
Bug is in JSON-to-BSON encoding producing wrong array key names ('1','3' vs '0','1'), a BSON serialization issue, not a CRUD API shape or semantics problem.
Pure documentation link update pointing collation method docs to the final 3.4 manual page; no spec behavior involved.
collection::find() existed but ignored the allow_partial_results and no_cursor_timeout find options, a CRUD option-semantics violation where the option is silently dropped rather than applied.
Documentation link update for views page in 3.4 manual; pure doc change with no spec involvement.

distinct() with collation should error when wire version < 5; the CRUD spec governs distinct() option semantics and collation handling, and the existing implementation failed to raise the required error.
Bug is about BSON regex flag serialization ordering (alphabetical), a BSON encoding rule, not a CRUD API shape or semantics issue.

distinct() exists but ignores the read_preference option; the CRUD spec governs distinct's option semantics and the option was not being applied, making this a conformance bug in an existing operation.
Resolution was to document the limitation, not fix behavior; Sonnet correctly identified this as a doc-only change, not a CRUD spec nonconformance bug.
Memory leak of mongoc_cursor_t on error paths; pure resource-management implementation bug with no CRUD spec rule violation in result shape or semantics.

aggregate() ignores the user-supplied read_preference option and uses the collection's instead — this is an option-semantics violation of the CRUD aggregate operation, not a server-selection routing rule per se. The operation existed and behaved incorrectly.
Bug is about BSON timestamp encoding—t and i fields swapped when serializing b_timestamp via bson_append_timestamp—a BSON serialization issue, not a CRUD API shape or semantics violation.
Backport of fix for b_timestamp t/i fields appended in wrong order to bson_append_timestamp — a BSON serialization bug, not a CRUD result-shape or semantics issue.
This concerns the Read and Write Concern spec's handling of unknown read concern level strings (acknowledge_string documentation), not a CRUD operation, option, or result-shape rule.

Collection.bulkWrite() failed to inherit the collection's write concern, so the operation used wrong write concern semantics — a CRUD bulkWrite behavioral conformance bug, not a malformed writeConcern document being sent.
Documentation site tooling fix for Hugo 'ref' shortcode syntax; no driver spec involvement whatsoever.
Bug is about disabling causal consistency in implicit sessions, which is governed by the sessions spec, not CRUD result shapes or operation semantics.
Build configuration fix updating the minimum libmongoc version in CMakeLists.txt; no CRUD or spec behavior involved.
CMake build failure finding C driver packages; a build-system issue with no CRUD spec behavior involved.
Infinite loop in with_transaction callback retry logic; governed by transactions spec error-handling behavior, not CRUD result shape.
Bug is about not appending read/write concern to Atlas Search Index helper commands (index management spec), not about CRUD operation result shapes or semantics.
OID hex string constructor fails to copy the parsed bytes (missing memcpy), a BSON ObjectId encoding/parsing bug, not a CRUD API shape or semantics issue.
find() accepted the sort option but silently ignored it; the CRUD spec defines sort as a find option that must be applied, so existing find behavior violated the spec.
API ergonomics refactor changing a C++ constructor from bool to enum for readability; no CRUD spec rule governs this language-specific API shape.

ReadConcern inheritance from client/database/collection parents is governed by the read-concern spec, not the CRUD spec; this is about ReadConcern configuration propagation, not CRUD operation API/result shapes.
BulkWriteResult.upsertedIds is a CRUD spec result-shape field; the driver's implementation returned malformed data causing exceptions, a conformance bug in populating the result object.
find() accepts the comment option (CRUD spec option) but the driver never sends it to the server; the option existed in the API but behaved incorrectly by being ignored.

User passed a replacement-style document (no atomic operators) to update_one; per CRUD spec update_one requires atomic operators and replace_one prohibits them. The resolution (use replace_one) confirms the driver behaves per spec, but the underlying topic is CRUD update-document validation; not_a_bug is also arguable since it was closed as working-as-intended.
This is a test code fix to use $set with update_one to avoid a warning; the driver behavior is correct (update_one requires $ operators per CRUD spec). No spec nonconformance in the driver.

cursor_type is a find() option defined by the CRUD spec; the driver had find() implemented but ignored/dropped this spec-defined option, a result-shape/option-semantics conformance bug rather than cursor iteration mechanics.
CMake/pkg_config build configuration issue finding libbson on Windows; resolved by setting PKG_CONFIG_PATH. No spec involvement.

insertMany mutates the caller's options object, which violates the CRUD spec principle of not improperly handling/transforming user-provided options; this is a behavioral bug in an existing insertMany implementation.
JavaScript heap OOM when inserting millions of documents; a client-side memory/performance issue that the reporter resolved by buffering, with no CRUD spec rule violated.

ReadPreference passed as a class to MongoClient.connect was not honored due to URI/options parsing, causing reads to hit primary instead of the specified secondary—a server-selection routing issue, not a CRUD result-shape problem.
Third-party license documentation update (adding COPYING/THIRD-PARTY-NOTICES, relicensing files); purely legal/licensing, no CRUD spec involvement.

User was inserting _id values of different BSON types (ObjectID vs String), which are legitimately distinct keys; the driver and server behave correctly per spec, and the resolution points to document validation as the user-side fix.
listCollections ignores read preference and is routed incorrectly; listCollections is not a CRUD-spec operation, and the root cause is read-preference/server-selection handling.
Bug is about js-bson not supporting the 'x' regex option in BSON serialization; this is a BSON spec compliance issue, not a CRUD result/option shape issue.

[manual correction] findOne with a projection (fields) option works with two args but fails when an explicit undefined/null is passed as the third argument; this is language-level input coercion of the findOne API arguments rather than a CRUD result-shape or option-semantics rule, so confidence is low.
ignoreUndefined is a Node-specific BSON serialization option controlling how undefined values are encoded; this is not a CRUD spec rule but a language-level input/serialization behavior.

bulkWrite() silently resolves with a result instead of surfacing the error (e.g. writeErrors with duplicate key errors), which violates the CRUD spec's requirement to expose write errors from bulkWrite; existing implementation behaved incorrectly.
Bug is about cursor iteration mechanics (hasNext() behavior on an exhausted cursor), not about the find/aggregate call shape or CRUD result type; this is cursor lifecycle behavior.
Resolved as a documentation fix (updated docs to reflect actual return type); not a CRUD spec nonconformance.
Documentation fix to installation guide specifying npm version number; no driver behavior or CRUD spec involved.
Reporter confirmed this is not a bug (resume config error on user side) and it concerns change streams, not the CRUD spec.
User's own test concurrency issue (Jest async test ordering), resolved by --runInBand; not a driver spec nonconformance.

Bug is about SessionPool tracking ServerSessions and sending endSessions on topology close, which is session lifecycle management governed by the sessions spec, not CRUD result shape or operation semantics.

dropDatabase was sent with readPreference=nearest causing 'not master'; the fix forces primary read preference, which is a server-selection/routing rule, not a CRUD result-shape issue. dropDatabase is also not a CRUD spec operation.
Bug is about Extended JSON decoding of $date values returning current time instead of decoded value; this is Extended JSON/BSON serialization, not a CRUD API shape or semantics issue.
aggregate() ignored user-provided batchSize option and always defaulted to 1000; honoring the batchSize option for aggregate is a CRUD spec option-semantics violation in existing code.
Bug is about getMore not sending the same lsid as the initial find with non-primary readPreference; the fix adds a sessions prose test ensuring find+getMore share the same session id.
Bug is that txnNumber (retry machinery) is incorrectly attached to find/read commands; the retryable-writes spec restricts txnNumber to write operations only. Not a CRUD result-shape issue.

count() is sent to the primary instead of inheriting the SECONDARY read preference; the bug is about routing the operation to the wrong server, governed by the server-selection/read-preference rules, not CRUD result shape.

The bug is that the driver throws an error for multi=true updates instead of silently not applying retryable writes; this is retry-machinery behavior (which writes are retryable), not a CRUD result-shape or option-semantics violation.
Test helper for normalizing function string representations across browser/node environments; an internal test utility with no CRUD spec involvement.

ignoreUndefined is a driver-specific BSON serialization option controlling whether undefined values are encoded; it is not mandated by the CRUD spec. The bug is about serializer behavior, not CRUD result shape or option semantics.

find() applies the collation option to the command without the maxWireVersion check that decorateWithCollation enforces; the existing find implementation mishandles a CRUD option, a conformance bug in the find operation's option semantics.
pickNearest sorts servers randomly instead of by lastIsMasterMS proximity, violating read-preference nearest routing in the server-selection spec; not a CRUD result-shape issue.

The readPreference option provided to count() is not applied, so the operation is routed to the primary instead of the secondary — a server-selection/read-preference routing violation, not a CRUD result-shape issue.

Internal refactoring fixing how db_ops calls collection.find() with incorrect parameters; this is an internal API usage bug in addUser/db_ops helpers, not a CRUD spec rule violation in the caller-facing find() API.

Root cause was bson-ext serializer applying checkKeys to $clusterTime field, rejecting the '$' prefix during BSON serialization; fix was a bson-ext patch to ignore that key. This is a BSON serialization bug, not a CRUD result-shape issue.

The driver incorrectly applies the retryWrites field to the createIndexes command, which is not a supported retryable write operation per the retryable-writes spec; the fix only sets retryWrites for valid operations.
Root cause is BSON UTF-8 deserialization incorrectly rejecting valid 0xFFFD codepoint encoding; this is a BSON serialization bug, not a CRUD result-shape violation.

[manual correction] Collection.bulkWrite batch-splitting miscalculates BSON size by ignoring array index key bytes, causing batches to exceed maxBsonObjectSize and fail; batch-splitting is governed by the CRUD spec's bulk write semantics.
Bug is about the wire-protocol OP_REPLY CURSOR_NOT_FOUND response flag value, not a CRUD result shape or API issue.
Bug is about the MongoDB handshake spec (isMaster on every socket), a connection-establishment/wire-protocol concern, not a CRUD result-shape or operation-semantics violation.

Bulk operation ignores the session passed at initialization, so writes execute outside the started transaction; this violates session/transaction propagation rules, not CRUD result-shape or operation semantics.
Root cause is BSON size miscalculation (calculateObjectSize not respecting ignoreUndefined), a BSON serialization-level bug affecting batch splitting, not a CRUD result-shape or semantics violation.
Bug is about bson-ext incorrectly rejecting valid reserved $-prefixed keys during BSON serialization/validation, a BSON encoding issue, not a CRUD API shape or semantics violation.

The bug is that unordered bulk ops run batches in parallel while sharing a session, breaking retryable-writes/transactions which require serial session use; the root cause is retry machinery misbehaving, not a CRUD result-shape violation.
Bug concerns user BSON options leaking into initial connection handshake/auth parsing, which is a connection establishment/auth concern, not a CRUD operation result-shape or semantics violation.
Bug is about BSON Timestamp MAX_VALUE encoding using Int32 max instead of UInt32 max for high bits; this is a BSON value semantics issue, not a CRUD API shape/result violation.
Resolution was a documentation update of the existing ops array, not a CRUD result-shape conformance fix; no CRUD spec rule violated.
User-reported performance issue (slow inserts/updates), no spec rule violation; this is a support request, not a CRUD nonconformance.
Bug is about TransientTransactionError label being applied outside transaction context, a transactions-spec rule violation; not a CRUD result-shape issue.
Staleness calculation error affecting secondary/secondaryPreferred read preference selection is a server-selection spec violation, not a CRUD result-shape or operation-semantics issue.
Bug is that readPreference=secondary is ignored and find() routes to the primary instead of a secondary, which is a server-selection/read-preference routing violation, not a CRUD result-shape issue.
Bug is about Session.endSession not awaiting the implicit abortTransaction, a session/transaction lifecycle cleanup issue, not a CRUD result-shape or operation-semantics violation.
ignoreUndefined is a Node.js-specific language-level input handling option, not a CRUD spec option; the CRUD spec does not define ignoreUndefined behavior, so this is a driver-specific feature bug.

Commit states 'The bulk spec requires that the driver report the index of a failed operation according to its input to the unordered bulk operation'; the WriteError.index field in the BulkWriteResult is a CRUD result-shape requirement that existed but behaved incorrectly.
Bug is that RTT was not considered during server selection, so nearest readPreference selected servers randomly instead of by latency—a server-selection spec violation, not CRUD.

Root cause is monitoring connections sharing the pool with operations, causing a blackholed connection to starve the pool and prevent server cycling/abort—an SDAM/connection-pool and server-selection issue, not a CRUD result-shape or semantics violation.

Root cause is a connection-pool wait queue bug (CMAP) where failed connection creation left operations pending indefinitely; bufferMaxEntries is a legacy connection buffering option not governed by the CRUD spec.

Bug is about driver failing to route 'nearest' read to an eligible alternate server when the preferred server is down—a server-selection/read-preference routing issue, not a CRUD result-shape or semantics violation.

Regression in SDAM error handling disabled retryable writes against MongoDB 4.2+, causing 'not master' errors instead of automatic retry; this is broken retry machinery, not a CRUD result-shape violation.
Documentation typo fix for collation option description in aggregate; no driver behavior or spec conformance change.
Bug is about client metadata platform field sent during connection handshake (handshake/CMAP spec), unrelated to CRUD operation API shapes or results.
This corrects an example documentation file (test/examples/update_documents.js) that misused update operators in replaceOne; it is a docs/example fix, not a driver behavior nonconformance.
Bug is about connection-string parsing of readPreferenceTags affecting server selection by tag set, not a CRUD result-shape or operation semantics issue.
Bug is in readPreferenceServerSelector failing to short-circuit on Unknown topology, a server-selection routing rule, not a CRUD result-shape issue.
Bug is about EJSON strict parsing storing $numberInt value as a string rather than Int32, an Extended JSON/BSON encoding issue, not a CRUD API shape or semantics problem.
maxStaleness reducer crashes on empty server set; this is the server-selection spec's staleness algorithm, not a CRUD result-shape or operation issue.
The driver ignores the journal=true URI option when constructing the write concern; this is about parsing the connection string into a write concern document, not a CRUD result-shape violation.
Bug is about RTT calculation per server selection spec quote; not a CRUD result-shape or operation issue. Sonnet's classification is correct.
Reporter self-closed as invalid (was looking at wrong docs version); no actual defect, ticket resolved as not a real bug.

Bug is the getMore wire command sending cursor id as a double (656190150596979.0) instead of int64/long, which the server rejects; this is a wire-protocol field-type violation, not a CRUD API shape issue.

db.command incorrectly inherited readPreference from parent, routing admin commands to a secondary; this is a server-selection/read-preference routing issue, not a CRUD result-shape or operation-semantics violation.
Driver does not support the explain option for aggregate; this adds explain support for the first time rather than correcting existing behavior. Explain is a CRUD operation option.
Bug is about ReadPreference option extraction (maxStalenessSeconds, hedge), which is governed by the server-selection/read-preference spec, not CRUD result shapes or operation semantics.
Dependency version bump for the 'bl' package due to a security vulnerability; no CRUD spec involvement.
Bug is about how Long integer values are serialized/encoded as doubles in BSON, a serialization encoding issue, not a CRUD API shape or result-type violation.

[manual correction] This is a JavaScript-specific input coercion issue (whether bulkWrite applies toBSON before the atomic-operator validation check), which the CRUD spec explicitly does not govern; language-level input handling is out of scope, so Sonnet's dismissal is reasonable.
Bug is about Cursor.max/min cursor modifier methods accepting a document parameter (index bounds), which is cursor-modifier behavior, not a CRUD spec result-shape or operation-option rule.

The bug is that a transaction's read preference (from session) MUST override user read preferences but ReadPreference#resolve fails to pull it from the session—a transaction read-preference invariant, not a CRUD result/option violation.

[manual correction] find()'s noCursorTimeout option was incorrectly translated (timeout option set noCursorTimeout to its value instead of negation), a CRUD option-semantics violation in an existing find() implementation.
Bundling/packaging issue: driver cannot read package.json at runtime when bundled with webpack/parcel; not a CRUD spec conformance issue.
ignoreUndefined is a Node.js driver-specific BSON serialization option controlling whether undefined values are encoded; not a CRUD spec API/result-shape rule.

[manual correction] The spec-compliant throw on empty insertMany already occurs; this ticket only improves the error message wording, which is not a spec result-shape or behavior nonconformance. Tagging crud/conformance per closest match but it is effectively a cosmetic message improvement.
Bug is that readPreference is not parsed from the connection string URI; this is a connection-string spec violation, not a CRUD result-shape or operation-semantics issue.

Bug is about implicit session lifecycle—cloned cursor reusing an ended session violates the sessions spec rule that implicit sessions must be returned to the pool, not a CRUD result-shape or operation-semantics violation.
Bug is in Decimal128.fromString producing incorrect binary encoding for negative numbers; this is BSON serialization, not CRUD API shape or semantics.
cursor.setReadPreference(SECONDARY) is ignored and the read is routed to primary instead of a secondary; this is a read-preference routing violation, not a CRUD result-shape issue.
Bug is a BSON type loss during serialize/deserialize of topologyVersion.counter (int vs long) in SDAM heartbeat monitoring; not a CRUD result-shape or operation issue.
Bug is about BSON deserialization mishandling the __proto__ key, corrupting round-trip fidelity at the encoding/decoding level, not a CRUD API shape or semantics issue.
Bug is about BSON serialization of BSONRegExp options not being alphabetized, governed by the BSON spec and bson-corpus tests, not a CRUD API shape/semantics issue.

This is a TypeScript return-type ergonomics issue for distinct(); the actual runtime result shape is correct, so it's not a spec behavioral deviation. Prior classification was reasonable, but distinct() is a CRUD operation and the result typing is borderline—still essentially a language-specific typing concern, not a CRUD result-shape nonconformance.
TypeScript type annotation fix for the 'session' option on insertOne; no behavioral spec deviation, just a typing correction.
Bug is in the retryable-writes retryability determination (checking ServerType.Standalone vs TopologyType.Single), a retry-machinery rule violation, not a CRUD result-shape issue.
Bug is about EJSON serialization of non-finite doubles using $numberDouble, a BSON/Extended JSON encoding issue, not a CRUD API shape or semantics violation.
Performance regression caused by command monitoring being enabled by default and bson serialization slowdown; no CRUD spec rule violated, fixed in bson@4.5.0.

insertMany write errors omit errInfo.details that insertOne surfaces; the CRUD spec defines WriteError objects in results, and the existing insertMany implementation propagated them incorrectly, making this an existing-behavior conformance bug.

[manual correction] insertMany returns insertedCount=0 instead of the correct count when fullResponse is set, violating the CRUD-spec InsertManyResult shape (insertedCount must reflect inserted documents); the existing implementation behaved incorrectly.
Performance regression from duplicate getter loops in bulk write result construction; an internal performance bug, not a CRUD spec result-shape or semantics violation.

Root cause is BSON UTF-8 string deserialization rejecting valid server error message bytes; the fix is to skip UTF-8 validation when decoding server error strings, a BSON decoding issue not a CRUD result-shape violation.

Bug is about cursor getMore needing to run server selection to respect SDAM monitoring state transitions—a cursor/server-selection mechanics issue, not a CRUD result-shape or operation-semantics violation.
Bug is about BSON type constructors (Int32/Long/Double) coercing invalid string input instead of preserving/rejecting it; this is BSON value encoding, not CRUD API shape or semantics.
Bug is about ObjectId constructor input validation (BSON value handling), not a CRUD operation API shape or result. Correctly classified as bson, not crud.
Bug is about retrying commitTransaction/abortTransaction on RetryableWriteError label, a transaction lifecycle retry rule, not a CRUD result-shape issue.
Bug is about ReadPreference construction dropping hedge and maxStalenessSeconds options, which are read-preference/server-selection concerns, not CRUD result shapes or operation semantics.

readPreference parsed from connection string is not honored, affecting which server reads are routed to; this is connection-string/server-selection, not a CRUD result-shape or operation-semantics violation.
This is a TypeScript static return-type definition issue (find return type should include _id), not a CRUD spec behavior or result-shape nonconformance at runtime.
Bug is about retrying commitTransaction/abortTransaction per transaction lifecycle rules, governed by the transactions spec, not CRUD operation result shapes.
Bug is in ObjectId.isValid() validation logic for 12-char strings, which is BSON ObjectId encoding/validation, not a CRUD operation API, option, or result shape.
Bug is that the driver determines write retryability by error code instead of the RetryableWriteError label, a direct retryable-writes spec violation, not a CRUD result-shape issue.
Bug is about the mongodb-handshake spec: driver sends client metadata on retry handshakes when it should only appear in the first isMaster. This is wire-protocol/handshake, not CRUD.
CI test failures due to server v5.2 changing the aggregate explain response format ('stages' property no longer present); this is a test compatibility issue, not a driver spec nonconformance.
readPreferenceTags parsing produces empty objects, breaking read-preference tag-based server selection; this is a server-selection spec issue, not CRUD result/option shape.
TypeScript type-inference issue for _id with custom pkFactory; this is a language-specific typing concern, not a CRUD spec-defined behavior violation.
Resolution was a documentation update ('fixed docs'); the w:0 behavior was a server change and callbacks being required is expected, not a CRUD spec violation.
Bug is about client metadata/handshake platform string duplication (SPEC-579 handshake spec), not a CRUD operation result shape or semantics.

[manual correction] The comment option is a CRUD-spec operation option; sending it unconditionally to servers <4.4 violates the rule that optional fields/version-gating semantics be respected, causing an OperationFailure on existing operations.
Bug concerns comment option being sent to listIndexes/listDatabases/listCollections on servers <4.4, which are enumerate/index management operations, not CRUD spec operations.
Bug is about localThresholdMS default value (15ms vs 0ms), a server-selection spec rule governing routing latency window, not a CRUD result shape or operation semantics issue.
Implementation crash from a JS property getter conflict when wrapping a libmongocrypt error; no CRUD spec rule about result shape or semantics is violated.
Resolved as not-an-issue/working-as-designed; the maintainer explains the ops array reflects passed-in documents and suggests only a documentation improvement, not a spec nonconformance.
Bug is about how the value -0 is serialized into BSON (Double type 0x01), a BSON encoding issue, not a CRUD API shape or semantics issue.
Bug is about causal consistency in implicit sessions, governed by the sessions spec, not a CRUD result-shape or operation-semantics violation.
Writes (findAndModify, dropIndex) were routed to secondaries instead of primary; this is a routing/server-selection violation, not a CRUD result-shape or semantics bug.

Root cause is the monitoring connection's hello/streaming-protocol response matching logic (SDAM monitoring), causing the topology to keep a stepped-down node marked secondary; this is a server-monitoring/SDAM issue, not a CRUD result-shape or option violation.
Ticket concerns handling of the NoWritesPerformed error sequence in the retryable writes spec, not a CRUD result-shape or semantics violation.
Reported as a usage question about callback timing on first insert to a non-existent database; resolved as a user-education matter (pointed to docs/examples), not a CRUD spec nonconformance.
Bug is about BSON UTF-8 decoding validation (overlong encodings producing replacement chars instead of errors), a BSON serialization/deserialization concern, not a CRUD API shape or semantics issue.
Bug is about EJSON serialization of Double values losing precision in canonical format, a BSON encoding/serialization issue, not a CRUD API shape or semantics issue.
Bug is about Double.toExtendedJSON() producing malformed Extended JSON string ('1.7e+308.0'), an EJSON encoding issue, not a CRUD API shape or semantics violation.
Missing TypeScript callback type definition for insertOne in mongo-legacy typings; a type-declaration bug, not a CRUD spec behavior nonconformance.

Bug is that the driver omits lsid on read commands when writeConcern=0, violating the sessions spec rule that lsid MUST be sent on all commands except unacknowledged writes. Not a CRUD result-shape or semantics issue.

Root cause is sessions spec violation: lsid MUST be attached to reads even with writeConcern 0; only unacknowledged writes are exempt. The cursor getMore failure is downstream of the lsid omission, not a CRUD result-shape issue.
Decimal128.fromString produces wrong value for precision >34 decimal places; this is a BSON Decimal128 encoding/parsing bug, not a CRUD API shape or semantics issue.

Bug is in cursor.js result handling where a document property named 'result' collides with internal parsing during cursor iteration/toArray; this is cursor iteration mechanics, not CRUD find() API shape.
Bug is in EJSON.parse deserialization of canonical EJSON Date values when useBigInt64=true; this is BSON/EJSON encoding-decoding, not CRUD API shape or semantics.

Root cause is an internal _bsontype string check ('ObjectID' vs 'ObjectId') for identifying BSON ObjectId instances; this is a BSON type-detection bug, not a CRUD result-shape or option-semantics violation.

[manual correction] find()'s handling of an optional projection argument is CRUD API option semantics; passing undefined fields should be ignored rather than throwing, so the existing find implementation behaves incorrectly vs spec.

[manual correction] Collection.bulkWrite() crashes when the optional options argument is omitted ('Cannot read property ordered of undefined'); options must be optional per CRUD spec, so this is a conformance bug in existing bulkWrite code.
Ticket is a documentation addition for Collection.prototype.find, not a spec nonconformance or behavior change.
Bug is about BSON Binary.toString/toJSON not respecting the position property when converting bytes, a BSON serialization consistency issue, not a CRUD API result shape or semantics issue.

BulkWriteResult.insertedIds is a CRUD result-shape field; including IDs of documents that were not actually inserted (due to write errors) violates the spec's definition that each value is the id of the inserted document. Existing implementation behaves incorrectly, making this a conformance bug.

Root cause is BSON serialization of undefined values (omitted vs. serialized to null); fix introduced ignoreUndefined serialization option. This is a language-level/BSON encoding decision, not a CRUD result-shape or API rule violation.
This is a TypeScript type-checking issue with UpdateFilter when a schema extends Document (index signature), not a runtime CRUD spec behavior violation; fix is to type definitions and API comments.

[manual correction] Collection.bulkWrite() exists but fails to generate _id consistently via the configured pkFactory; CRUD spec requires the driver to generate _id when absent, and this corrects existing wrong behavior, though pkFactory itself is language-specific.
Bug is about unpinning connections when ending a session per load balancer/sessions spec, not a CRUD result-shape or operation semantics issue.
